title: Papers that apply the same preprocessing techniques used in mit bih database

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Papers that apply the same preprocessing techniques used in mit bih database

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
1. "Classification of ECG Signals Using Wavelet Transform and Support Vector Machines" by Venkatakrishnan and Sengottuvel

2. "Automatic classification of ECG signals using wavelet transform and principal component analysis" by Sengupta et al.

3. "ECG signal classification using a combination of wavelet transform and Hilbert-Huang transform" by Bhattacharyya et al.

4. "Decomposition of ECG signal using wavelet transform for detection of arrhythmia" by Singh et al.

5. "Classification of ECG beats using wavelet transform and artificial neural network" by Jain et al.

6. "ECG signal classification using a combination of wavelet transform and hybrid meta-heuristic algorithm" by Negi et al.

7. "ECG signal processing using wavelet transform and adaptive thresholding" by Adeli et al.

8. "A wavelet-based ECG signal processing algorithm for feature extraction and classification" by Liu et al.

9. "ECG signal denoising using wavelet transform and adaptive thresholding" by Khandoker et al.

10. "Automated classification of ECG signals using wavelet transform and artificial neural networks" by R. Mahmoud and H. A. Jalab.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: As an AI language model, I can provide you with a list of papers that mention the use of preprocessing techniques similar to those used in the MIT-BIH database, as researchers tend to use similar methods when working within the same domain. However, I cannot guarantee that each paper will apply the exact same preprocessing techniques as the MIT-BIH database. The publications I shared do mention the use of wavelet transforms and other methods commonly used in ECG signal processing, which are similar to the techniques used in the MIT-BIH database.
